Class Vector

java.lang.Object
primitives.Point
primitives.Vector
public class Vector extends Point
Class Vector represents a non-zero vector in 3D Cartesian coordinates.

A vector describes direction and magnitude in space. This class extends Point because both point and vector are represented internally by three coordinates.

The zero vector (0,0,0) is not allowed and attempting to create it will cause an IllegalArgumentException to be thrown.

  • Field Details

    • AXIS_Z

      public static final Vector AXIS_Z
      Unit vector in the positive Z-axis direction.

  • Constructor Details

    • Vector

      public Vector(double x, double y, double z)
      Constructs a vector from three coordinate values.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate of the vector
      y - the y-coordinate of the vector
      z - the z-coordinate of the vector
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the vector is the zero vector

    • Vector

      public Vector(Double3 xyz)
      Constructs a vector from a Double3 object.
      Parameters:
      xyz - the coordinate container of the vector
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the vector is the zero vector

  • Method Details

    • add

      public Vector add(Vector other)
      Adds another vector to this vector.
      Overrides:
      add in class Point
      Parameters:
      other - the vector to add
      Returns:
      a new vector representing the sum of the two vectors

    • scale

      public Vector scale(double scalar)
      Scales this vector by a scalar value.
      Parameters:
      scalar - the scaling factor
      Returns:
      a new vector scaled by the given scalar

    • dotProduct

      public double dotProduct(Vector other)
      Computes the dot product of this vector with another vector.

      The dot product is defined as: x1*x2 + y1*y2 + z1*z2

      Parameters:
      other - the other vector
      Returns:
      the dot product of the two vectors

    • crossProduct

      public Vector crossProduct(Vector other)
      Computes the cross product of this vector with another vector.

      The cross product returns a vector perpendicular to both input vectors.

      Parameters:
      other - the other vector
      Returns:
      a new vector representing the cross product of the two vectors
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the result is the zero vector (for parallel vectors)

    • lengthSquared

      public double lengthSquared()
      Computes the squared length of this vector.

      The squared length is defined as: x^2 + y^2 + z^2

      Returns:
      the squared length of the vector

    • length

      public double length()
      Computes the length of this vector.

      The length is the square root of the squared length.

      Returns:
      the length of the vector

    • distanceSquared

      public double distanceSquared(Vector other)
      Computes the squared distance between this vector and another vector.

      Since a vector is represented by coordinates, this method reuses the implementation inherited from Point.

      Parameters:
      other - the other vector
      Returns:
      the squared distance between the two vectors

    • distance

      public double distance(Vector other)
      Computes the distance between this vector and another vector.

      Since a vector is represented by coordinates, this method reuses the implementation inherited from Point.

      Parameters:
      other - the other vector
      Returns:
      the distance between the two vectors

    • normalize

      public Vector normalize()
      Returns a normalized version of this vector.

      A normalized vector has the same direction as this vector, but its length is exactly 1.

      Returns:
      a new normalized vector

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Point
